title: koeleria phleoides subvar. robusta
intro: Koeleria phleoides subvar. robusta, also known as Koeler's grass or robust Koeler's grass, is a subspecies of the Koeleria phleoides plant, a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Koeleria phleoides subvar. robusta has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Koeleria phleoides subvar. robusta include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, Koeleria phleoides subvar. robusta is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a variety of ailments.
